150101.english: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, which is our hope;
150101.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᏉᎳ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᎩᏅᏏᏛ ᎬᎩᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎦᏤᎵᎦ,
150101.phonetic: A-yv Quo-la Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv a-gi-nv-si-dv gv-gi-ne-tse-lv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi i-gi-s-de-li-s-gi a-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv na-s-gi u-du-gi gv-di ge-sv i-ga-tse-li-ga,

150102.english: Unto Timothy, my own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.
150102.cherokee: ᏫᎬᏲᏪᎳᏏ ᏘᎹᏗ ᎠᏋᏒ ᎠᏆᏤᎵ ᎠᏊᏥ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ; ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏕᏣᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᎦᏤᎵᎦ.
150102.phonetic: wi-gv-yo-we-la-si Ti-ma-di a-quv-sv a-qua-tse-li a-quu-tsi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv; gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv a-le u-da-do-li-tsa-dv ge-sv a-le nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv de-tsa-ka-hna-wa-di-do-he-s-di nv-da-yu-na-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi I-gi-do-da a-le Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-ga-tse-li-ga.

150103.english: As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,
150103.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᏥᎬᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᏣᏗᎩᏯᏍᏗᏱ ᎡᏈᏌ, ᎹᏏᏙᏁ ᏥᏩᎩᎶᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏘᏅᏍᏓᏕᏗᏱ ᎩᎶ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᎢᏳᏂᏪᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ,
150103.phonetic: Na-no tsi-gv-ta-yo-se-lv-gi tsa-di-gi-ya-s-di-yi E-qui-sa, Ma-si-do-ne tsi-wa-gi-lo-sv-gi, na-s-gi ti-nv-s-da-de-di-yi gi-lo da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gv u-tsa-ti-ni i-yu-ni-we-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na,

150104.english: Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in faith: so do.
150104.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏛᏓᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏎᏉ ᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏫᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎧᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏓᏁᏟᏴᏒᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗᏉ ᏥᏩᎵᏱᎶᎯᎭ ᎥᏲᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏁᎳᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏛᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏨᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ, [ᎾᏍᎩ ᎿᎭᏛᏁᎸᎭ.]
150104.phonetic: A-le u-na-dv-da-s-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na a-se-quo ga-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv a-le wi-na-s-dv-na ka-no-he-di ge-sv u-na-da-ne-tli-yv-sv-sv-i, na-s-gi ga-wo-ni-hi-s-di-quo tsi-wa-li-yi-lo-hi-ha v-yo-no u-ne-la-nv-hi di-ne-la-do-di ge-sv u-dv-hi-s-di-yi, na-s-gi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv tsv-di-da-le-hv-s-ga, [na-s-gi hna-dv-ne-lv-ha.]

150105.english: Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned:
150105.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᎨᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎣᎾᏫ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏠᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
150105.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-da-ge-yu-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-la na-s-gi ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na o-na-wi nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi, a-le o-s-dv di-gu-go-do-di ge-sv a-le nu-tlo-na-s-dv-na go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i;

150106.english: From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;
150106.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎩᎶ ᎤᎾᎪᎸᏛ ᏥᎩ, ᏄᎵᏌᎶᏛᎾ ᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏭᎾᎦᏔᎲᏍᏔᏅ;
150106.phonetic: Na-s-gi na-hna gi-lo u-na-go-lv-dv tsi-gi, nu-li-sa-lo-dv-na ga-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv wu-na-ga-ta-hv-s-ta-nv;

150107.english: Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm.
150107.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏗᏃᏏᏏᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏃᎵᎬᎾ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
150107.phonetic: U-na-du-li-s-gv-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di di-no-si-si-s-gi i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi, a-se-no na-no-li-gv-na nu-s-dv a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv, a-le nu-s-dv a-ni-s-da-yi-di-s-gv-i.

150108.english: But we know that the law is good, if a man use it lawfully;
150108.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏗᎦᏔᎭ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎨᏒ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᏱᏓᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎦ,
150108.phonetic: A-se-no i-di-ga-ta-ha di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di o-si-yu ge-sv, i-yu-no gi-lo u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya yi-da-ka-hna-wa-de-ga,

150109.english: Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,
150109.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏯᎦᏔᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎪᏢᎾᏁᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᏂᏚᎾᏁᎶᏛᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏒᎾ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᏚᎾᏁᎶᏛᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᎢ, ᎠᏂᎦᏓᎭᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏂᏆᏘᎯ, ᏧᏂᏙᏓ ᏗᏂᎯᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᏥ ᏗᏂᎯᎯ, ᏴᏫ ᏗᏂᎯᎯ,
150109.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-quo ya-ga-ta-ha, na-s-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di u-da-nv-ti yv-wi a-go-tlv-na-ne-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-s-gi-ni ni-du-na-ne-lo-dv-na, a-le nu-no-hi-yu-sv-na, u-ne-la-nv-hi ni-du-na-ne-lo-dv-na, a-le a-ni-s-ga-na-i, a-ni-ga-da-ha-i, a-le o-s-dv ge-sv a-ni-ni-qua-ti-hi, tsu-ni-do-da di-ni-hi-hi a-le tsu-ni-tsi di-ni-hi-hi, yv-wi di-ni-hi-hi,

150110.english: For whoremongers, for them that defile themselves with mankind, for menstealers, for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine;
150110.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ, ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏗᎾᏂᏏᎲᏍᎩ, ᏴᏫ ᏗᏂᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩ, ᎠᏂᏰᎪᎩ, ᎦᏰᎪᎩ ᎠᎾᏎᎵᏛᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᏘᎴᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
150110.phonetic: U-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv, a-ni-s-ga-ya di-na-ni-si-hv-s-gi, yv-wi di-ni-no-s-gi-s-gi, a-ni-ye-go-gi, ga-ye-go-gi a-na-se-li-dv-s-gi, a-le na-s-quo ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di na-s-gi di-ga-ti-le-gi o-s-dv di-de-yo-di ge-sv-i;

150111.english: According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.
150111.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎥᏆᏒᎦᎶᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ.
150111.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya nu-s-dv ga-lv-quo-di-yu o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv e-hi u-tse-li-ga; na-s-gi a-yv v-qua-sv-ga-lo-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi.

150112.english: And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath enabled me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;
150112.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᏯᎵᎡᎵᏤᎭ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᏣᏆᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏔᏅ, ᏰᎵᏉ ᎢᎬᏩᏛᏁᏗ ᏣᏇᎵᏎᎸ, ᏣᎩᏁᏤᎸ ᏗᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ,
150112.phonetic: A-le tsi-ya-li-e-li-tse-ha Ga-lo-ne-dv Tsi-sa U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-ga-tse-li tsa-qua-li-ni-go-hi-s-ta-nv, ye-li-quo i-gv-wa-dv-ne-di tsa-que-li-se-lv, tsa-gi-ne-tse-lv di-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi a-li-tsi-do-di ge-sv-i,

150113.english: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.
150113.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏥᏐᏢᏗᏍᎩ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏗᎬᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏂᏐᏢᏗ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᎩᏙᎵᏨᎩ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏂᏥᎦᏔᎿᎭᎥᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏉᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏆᏛᏁᎸᎢ.
150113.phonetic: A-yv na-s-gi i-lv-hi-yu tsi-so-tlv-di-s-gi tsi-ge-sv-gi, a-le u-yo i-di-gv-ne-hi, a-le di-ga-ni-so-tlv-di; a-se-no v-gi-do-li-tsv-gi, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi ni-tsi-ga-ta-hna-v-na ge-sv-i, a-le na-quo-hi-yu-sv-na ge-sv na-s-gi na-qua-dv-ne-lv-i.

150114.english: And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
150114.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᎦᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎤᎵᏠᏯᏍᏛᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏥᏲᎢᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏥᎨᏳᎢ, ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150114.phonetic: A-le gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-ga-tse-li-ga u-tsa-ta-nv-hi e-quo-hi-yu ge-sv-gi, u-li-tlo-ya-s-dv-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv Tsi-sa tsi-yo-i-yu-di ge-sv a-le tsi-ge-yu-i, i-yu-li-s-do-di ge-sv-i.

150115.english: This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.
150115.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᏪᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏩᏍᏛ ᏧᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗᏳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏥᏌ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎷᏨᎢ, ᏚᏍᏕᎸᎯᎸ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᎢ, ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎩᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏥᏍᎦᎾᎢ.
150115.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a na-s-gi i-ga-we-dv, a-le wa-s-dv tsu-da-ni-lv-i-s-di-yu, na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv Tsi-sa e-lo-hi u-lu-tsv-i, du-s-de-lv-hi-lv a-ni-s-ga-na-i, a-yv na-s-gi na-gi-gv-wi-yu-sv tsi-s-ga-na-i.

150116.english: Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.
150116.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎥᎩᏙᎵᏨᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎨᏒ ᎾᎩᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏩᏁᏗᏱ ᏩᏍᏛ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏟᎶᏍᏙᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎣᏂ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᏅᎯ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏫᏚᎷᎩ.
150116.phonetic: A-se-no na-s-gi hi-a nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi v-gi-do-li-tsv-gi; na-s-gi a-yv ge-sv na-gi-gv-wi-yu-sv Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-wa-ne-di-yi wa-s-dv gv-ni-di-yu ge-sv-i, na-s-gi tsu-na-tli-lo-s-do-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi o-ni gv-wo-hi-yu-nv-hi gv-ni-dv a-li-s-qua-di-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na wi-du-lu-gi.

150117.english: Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.
150117.cherokee: Ꭷ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᏲᎱᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎤᏩᏒᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎢ; ᎡᎺᏅ.
150117.phonetic: Ka, U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-li-s-qua-di-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv e-hi, a-yo-hu-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, a-go-wa-dv-di ni-ge-sv-na, u-wa-sv-hi a-ga-ta-hna-i U-ne-la-nv-hi, ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se-s-di a-le ga-lv-quo-do-di ge-se-s-di ni-go-hi-lv a-le ni-go-hi-lv-i; E-me-nv.

150118.english: This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest wage a good warfare;
150118.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎯ ᏕᎬᏲᎯᏏ, ᎠᏇᏥ ᏗᎹᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏂᎯ ᎨᏣᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᎾ ᏕᎲᏗᏍᎬ ᏯᎵᎦ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
150118.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv ni-hi de-gv-yo-hi-si, a-que-tsi Di-ma-di, na-s-gi-ya nu-ni-we-sv u-na-do-le-ho-sv ni-hi ge-tsa-no-he-s-gv-i, na-s-gi-no ni-hi na-s-gi-na de-hv-di-s-gv ya-li-ga o-s-dv a-li-s-di ge-sv-i;

150119.english: Holding faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck:
150119.cherokee: ᏕᏣᏂᏴᏒ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏩᏂᏲᏥᏙᎸ;
150119.phonetic: De-tsa-ni-yv-sv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le o-s-dv di-gu-go-do-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi gi-lo i-yv-dv i-yu-nv-ne-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi, na-s-gi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-wa-ni-yo-tsi-do-lv;

150120.english: Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.
150120.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎸ ᎭᎻᏂᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎵᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏥᏲᏒ ᏎᏓᏂ ᏕᏥᏲᎯᏎᎸ ᎤᎾᏕᎶᏆᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏐᏢᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150120.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a nu-na-dv-ne-lv Ha-mi-ni-ya a-le E-li-gi, na-s-gi de-ga-tsi-yo-sv Se-da-ni de-tsi-yo-hi-se-lv u-na-de-lo-qua-s-di-yi u-ni-so-tlv-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

150201.english: I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men;
150201.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏥᏁᎦ ᎢᎬᏱᏱᏉ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏔᏲᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏗᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ,
150201.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no tsi-ne-ga i-gv-yi-yi-quo ge-sv-i, a-ta-yo-s-di ge-sv-i, a-da-do-li-s-do-di ge-sv-i, di-nv-da-di-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-li-he-li-s-di ge-sv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi, na-s-gi na-ni-v yv-wi u-gv-wa-li,

150202.english: For kings, and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.
150202.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏄᎾᏰᎯᏍᏛᎾ ᎢᎦᎴᏂᏓᏍᏗᏱ, ᏕᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏒ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150202.phonetic: U-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi a-le na-ni-v a-ye-li tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi u-gv-wa-li, na-s-gi nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv a-le nu-na-ye-hi-s-dv-na i-ga-le-ni-da-s-di-yi, de-di-ka-hna-wa-di-sv du-yu-go-dv ge-sv a-le ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv-i.

150203.english: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;
150203.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗᏳ ᏓᎧᏅ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ,
150203.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no hi-a o-si-yu a-le tsu-da-ni-lv-i-s-di-yu da-ka-nv U-ne-la-nv-hi I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi,

150204.english: Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.
150204.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᎨᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150204.phonetic: Na-s-gi o-si-yu u-ye-lv-di tsi-gi na-ni-v yv-wi ge-tsi-s-de-lv-di-yi, a-le u-ni-lu-hi-s-di-yi a-ga-do-v-hi-s-di ge-sv du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i.

150205.english: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;
150205.cherokee: ᏌᏉᏉᏰᏃ ᎡᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᏉ ᏗᎪᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏗᎴᏁᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏴᏫ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ;
150205.phonetic: Sa-quo-quo-ye-no e-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi, a-le sa-quo di-go-hi-s-di-s-gi a-ye-li di-le-ne-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-le yv-wi, na-s-gi na yv-wi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv;

150206.english: Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time.
150206.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏚᏓᏲᏎ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏥᏚᎫᏴᎮ ᎾᏂᎥᎢ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏁᏗ ᎠᏎᎸᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᎯᏳᎢ,
150206.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsi-du-da-yo-se u-wa-sv tsi-du-gu-yv-he na-ni-v-i, gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-ne-di a-se-lv-hi ge-sv na-hi-yu-i,

150207.english: Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity.
150207.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏴ ᎥᎩᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᎦᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎥᎩᏅᏏᏛ, ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏗᎦᏥᏰᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏄᎮᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏰᎪᎩ ᏂᎬᏗᏍᎬᎾ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᏥᏁᎦ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎦᏥᎪᎥᏍᎦ.
150207.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a a-yv v-gi-ne-tse-lv-hi ga-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gi a-le v-gi-nv-si-dv, tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi di-ga-tsi-ye-yo-hv-s-gi ga-lo-nu-he-dv a-le ga-ye-go-gi ni-gv-di-s-gv-na; u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya tsi-ne-ga Ga-lo-ne-dv a-ga-ta-hv-i, v-tla yi-ga-tsi-go-v-s-ga.

150208.english: I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting.
150208.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏕᎨᏌᏗᏒ ᎤᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏱ, ᏧᏂᏌᎳᏙᏗᏱ ᏗᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᏄᏂᏔᎳᏬᏍᎬᎾ ᎠᎴ ᏄᎾᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎲᎾ.
150208.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-qua-du-li-ha a-ni-s-ga-ya ni-ga-v de-ge-sa-di-sv u-na-da-do-li-s-do-yi, tsu-ni-sa-la-do-di-yi di-ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na tsu-no-ye-ni nu-ni-ta-la-wo-s-gv-na a-le nu-na-dla-si-dv-e-hv-na.

150209.english: In like manner also, that women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;
150209.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏓᎾᏣᎤᏍᎬ ᎤᏢᏉᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏓᎾᏄᏬᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᎾᏓᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎥᏝᏍᏗᏃ ᏓᏂᏍᏕᏲᎲ ᏚᏂᏍᏘᏰᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏓᎶᏂᎨ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎬᎾ-ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏗᏄᏬ ᏱᏓᎾᏣᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ;
150209.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo a-ni-ge-yv da-na-tsa-u-s-gv u-tlv-quo-di ni-ge-sv-na da-na-nu-wo-s-ge-s-di, u-na-da-na-ye-hi-s-di a-le u-na-li-si-na-hi-s-di ge-se-s-di; v-tla-s-di-no da-ni-s-de-yo-hv du-ni-s-ti-ye-gv-i, a-le a-de-lv da-lo-ni-ge, a-le da-gv-na--i-yu-na-s-di, a-le u-tsa-ti tsu-gv-wa-lo-di di-nu-wo yi-da-na-tsa-nv-di-s-ge-s-di;

150210.english: But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.
150210.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏓᎾᏣᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏧᏂᎵᎶᎲᏍᎦ.
150210.phonetic: O-s-dv-s-gi-ni di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv da-na-tsa-nv-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi tsu-na-ne-lo-di a-ni-ge-yv tsu-ni-li-lo-hv-s-ga.

150211.english: Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.
150211.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎡᎳᏪᏱ ᎠᏕᎶᏆᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
150211.phonetic: A-le a-ge-yv e-la-we-yi a-de-lo-qua-s-ge-s-di u-tsa-ti u-wo-hi-yu-hi-yu ge-se-s-di.

150212.english: But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to usurp authority over the man, but to be in silence.
150212.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏱᏥᏰᎵᏎ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏧᏕᏲᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᎵᏁᎯᏕᏗᏱ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎡᎳᏪᏱᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏩᏗᏱ.
150212.phonetic: V-tla-s-gi-ni u-ne-la-gi yi-tsi-ye-li-se a-ge-yv tsu-de-yo-di-yi a-le u-da-li-ne-hi-de-di-yi a-s-ga-ya, e-la-we-yi-quo-s-gi-ni u-wa-di-yi.

150213.english: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.

150214.english: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.
150214.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎠᏓᏫ ᏯᏥᎶᏄᎡᎴᎢ, ᎠᎨᏴᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏥᎶᏄᎡᎸ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏤᎢ.
150214.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no A-da-wi ya-tsi-lo-nu-e-le-i, a-ge-yv-s-gi-ni a-tsi-lo-nu-e-lv u-s-ga-nv-tse-i.

150215.english: Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety.
150215.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏕᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎬᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏓᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏎᏍᏗ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᎨᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150215.phonetic: A-se-s-gi-ni-no-nv a-tsi-s-de-lv-di i-ge-se-s-di di-ni-yo-li de-ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-gv-i, i-yu-no da-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-se-s-di go-hi-yu-di ge-sv, a-le a-da-ge-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, a-le a-li-si-na-hi-s-do-di ge-sv-i.

150301.english: This is a true saying, If a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.
150301.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎢᎦᏪᏛ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏚᏝᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
150301.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya i-ga-we-dv; I-yu-no gi-lo u-du-tla-s-ge-s-di a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv u-du-li-s-ge-s-di.

150302.english: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour, given to hospitality, apt to teach;
150302.cherokee: ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎠᏗᎾ ᎠᏎ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏌᏉᏉ ᎠᏓᏰᎮᏍᏗ, ᎨᏯᏔᎯ, ᎤᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᏧᏓᏓᏂᎸᏣᏘ, ᏗᎬᏩᏕᏲᏗ;
150302.phonetic: A-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di a-di-na a-se go-hu-s-di na-s-ga-nv-na ge-se-s-di, sa-quo-quo a-da-ye-he-s-di, ge-ya-ta-hi, u-li-si-na-hi-s-di, o-s-dv tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi, tsu-da-da-ni-lv-tsa-ti, di-gv-wa-de-yo-di;

150303.english: Not given to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;
150303.cherokee: ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎤᎦᎾᏏᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᏗᏓᏛᏂᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᎬᎥᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏍᎩᏂ, ᏧᏘᏲᏌᏘ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᏕᎸ ᏧᎨᏳᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
150303.phonetic: Gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di u-ga-na-si-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, di-da-dv-ni-hi ni-ge-sv-na, tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-gv-v-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, u-da-nv-ti-s-gi-ni, tsu-ti-yo-sa-ti ni-ge-sv-na, a-de-lv tsu-ge-yu-hi ni-ge-sv-na.

150304.english: One that ruleth well his own house, having his children in subjection with all gravity;
150304.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏒ ᏚᏓᏘᎿᎭᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᏧᏪᏥ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᎯᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎢᎬᏁᎯ.
150304.phonetic: U-wa-sv du-da-ti-hna-v o-s-dv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi, tsu-we-tsi gv-wo-hi-yu-hi-yu a-le gv-wa-lv-quo-di-yu i-gv-ne-hi.

150305.english: (For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)
150305.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎾᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏚᏓᏘᎿᎭᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏓᏍᏗᏱ, ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏱᏓᎦᏌᏯᏍᏓ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ?
150305.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no gi-lo na-ga-ta-hv-na yi-gi u-wa-sv du-da-ti-hna-v o-s-dv tsu-ka-hna-wa-di-da-s-di-yi, ga-do yi-ga-li-s-do-da yi-da-ga-sa-ya-s-da tsu-na-ne-lo-hi u-na-da-tlu-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga?

150306.english: Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil.
150306.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎳᎢ ᎤᎵᎪᏃᏅᎯ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᏢᏉᏙᏗᏰᏃ ᎨᏒ ᏯᎵᏌᎳᏙᏓ ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏚᏚᎪᏓᏏ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᏚᏚᎪᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
150306.phonetic: A-le v-tle-s-di gi-la-i u-li-go-no-nv-hi yi-ge-se-s-di, a-tlv-quo-do-di-ye-no ge-sv ya-li-sa-la-do-da na-s-gi-no u-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi ge-sv yi-du-du-go-da-si na-s-gi-ya a-s-gi-na du-du-go-da-ne-lv-i.

150307.english: Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
150307.cherokee: ᎠᎴᏬ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏱᎩ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏅᎫᏛᎢ, ᎤᏐᏅᏰᏃ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏱᏅᎦᎵᏍᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏌᏛ ᏳᏪᎯ.
150307.phonetic: A-le-wo o-si-yu gv-wa-no-he-s-gi yi-gi i-yv-dv u-nv-gu-dv-i, u-so-nv-ye-no a-tsi-no-he-s-gi yi-nv-ga-li-s-da, a-le a-s-gi-na u-sa-dv yu-we-hi.

150308.english: Likewise must the deacons be grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;
150308.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᏯᏙᎯᎯ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏞᏍᏗ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎢᏯᏂᏪᏍᎩ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᎦᎨᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎦᎾᏏᏍᎩ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᏂᎬᎥᏍᎩ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ;
150308.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo a-ni-ya-do-hi-hi ge-tsi-lv-quo-di ge-se-s-di, v-tle-s-di tsu-da-le-nv-dv i-ya-ni-we-s-gi yi-ge-se-s-di, a-le v-tle-s-di yi-ga-ge-a-di-ta-s-di u-ni-ga-na-si-s-gi yi-ge-se-s-di, tle-s-di tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-ni-gv-v-s-gi yi-ge-se-s-di;

150309.english: Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
150309.cherokee: ᏚᏂᏂᏴᏎᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150309.phonetic: Du-ni-ni-yv-se-s-di-s-gi-ni u-de-li-dv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i a-nv-di-s-ge-s-di ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na di-gu-go-do-di ge-sv-i.

150310.english: And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being found blameless.
150310.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏗᎪᎵᏰᎥᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎩᎳ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏯᏙᎯᎲ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᎨᎫᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
150310.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi hi-a i-gv-yi di-go-li-ye-v-hi ge-se-s-di, gi-la du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-s-di a-ni-ya-do-hi-hv go-hu-s-di ga-ge-gu-i-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-se-s-di.

150311.english: Even so must their wives be grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.
150311.cherokee: ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᏧᎾᏓᎵᎢ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏐᏅ ᎠᎾᏓᏃᎮᎵᏙᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᏁᏯᏔᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏰᎵᎦᏯ ᎢᏯᎾᏛᏁᎯ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ.
150311.phonetic: Na-s-quo-no tsu-na-da-li-i ge-tsi-lv-quo-di ge-se-s-di, u-so-nv a-na-da-no-he-li-do-hi ni-ge-sv-na, a-ne-ya-ta-hi-s-gi-ni ge-se-s-di, ye-li-ga-ya i-ya-na-dv-ne-hi ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di.

150312.english: Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
150312.cherokee: ᎠᏯᏙᎯᎯ ᏌᏉᎭ ᎠᏓᏰᎮᏍᏗ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏚᏓᏘᎿᎭᎥᎢ.
150312.phonetic: A-ya-do-hi-hi sa-quo-ha a-da-ye-he-s-di, o-s-dv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi tsu-we-tsi a-le u-wa-sv du-da-ti-hna-v-i.

150313.english: For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
150313.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏯᏙᎯᎲᎢ, ᎠᏂᏩᏛᏗᎭ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᎦᏓᏅᏖᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏄᎾᏰᎯᏍᏛᎾ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏥᏌ.
150313.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no o-s-dv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi ge-sv a-ni-ya-do-hi-hv-i, a-ni-wa-dv-di-ha o-s-dv ge-ga-da-nv-te-di-yi, a-le u-tsa-ti nu-na-ye-hi-s-dv-na a-da-nv-da-di-s-di-yi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv Ga-lo-ne-dv Tsi-sa.

150314.english: These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:
150314.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏫᎬᏲᏪᎳᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎠᏋᎭ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎾᏉ ᏫᎬᎷᏤᏗᏱ;
150314.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a tsu-da-le-nv-dv wi-gv-yo-we-la-si, a-le u-du-gi a-quv-ha ni-go-hi-lv-na-quo wi-gv-lu-tse-di-yi;

150315.english: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
150315.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎠᏉᎯᏤᎵᏙᎸᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏗᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏥᎩ, ᎦᎫᏍᏛᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏱᏙ ᏥᎩ ᏚᏳᎯᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150315.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni-no-nv a-quo-hi-tse-li-do-lv-ha na-s-gi tsa-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi di-tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li a-da-ne-lv-i, na-s-gi tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv gv-ni-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li tsi-gi, ga-gu-s-dv-di a-le u-li-s-da-yi-do tsi-gi du-yu-hi-dv ge-sv-i.

150316.english: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.
150316.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏄᏜᏓᏏᏛᏒᎾ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏁᎶᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᎬᏁᎴ ᎤᏬᏓᎵ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏄᏠᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏩᏁᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᎪᎮᎢ, ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎨᎦᎵᏥᏙᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᎪᎢᏳᏁᎢ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏗᎨᏒ ᏫᏓᎦᏓᏂᎸᏤᎢ.
150316.phonetic: A-le nu-dla-si-dv-sv-na u-s-qua-ni-go-di-yu u-de-li-dv u-ne-la-nv-hi di-ne-lo-di ge-sv-i; U-ne-la-nv-hi gv-ni-ge-sv na-gv-ne-le u-wo-da-li ge-sv-i, nu-tlo-na-s-dv-na gv-ni-ge-sv nu-wa-ne-le A-da-nv-do, di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi gv-wa-go-he-i, tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi ge-ga-li-tsi-do-ne-le na-s-gi a-tsi-no-he-s-ge-i, a-ni e-lo-hi a-go-i-yu-ne-i, ga-lv-quo-di-yu di-ge-sv wi-da-ga-da-ni-lv-tse-i.

150401.english: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;
150401.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎬᏂᎨᏒᎢᏳ ᏂᎬᏁᎭ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏓᎾᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏠᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ,
150401.phonetic: A-se-no A-da-nv-do ka-ne-gv gv-ni-ge-sv-i-yu ni-gv-ne-ha u-li-s-qua-lv-di ge-se-s-di gi-lo tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, da-na-dv-da-s-da-ne-he-s-di u-na-tlo-na-s-di di-da-nv-do, a-le da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gi a-ni-s-gi-na,

150402.english: Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;
150402.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏠᎾᏍᏛ ᎦᏰᎪᎩ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏗᎦᎴᏴᏔᏅᎯ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᎤᏗᎴᎩ ᎬᏔᏅᎯ;
150402.phonetic: U-na-tlo-na-s-dv ga-ye-go-gi a-ni-wo-ni-s-ge-s-di, du-na-da-nv-dv di-ga-le-yv-ta-nv-hi ta-lu-gi-s-gi u-di-le-gi gv-ta-nv-hi;

150403.english: Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.
150403.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏅᏍᏙᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏗᏨᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏂᏁᎨᏍᏗ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᏗᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏬᏢᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨᎯᏉ ᏧᎾᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎦᏙᎥᏒᎯ ᏥᎩ ᏚᏳᎪᏛᎢ.
150403.phonetic: U-ni-nv-s-do-s-ge-s-di di-tsv-s-di-yi, a-ni-ne-ge-s-di go-hu-s-di i-yu-s-di a-li-s-da-yv-di di-yo-hi-s-di-yi na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-wo-tlv-nv-hi tsi-gi u-li-he-li-tsv-hi-quo tsu-na-da-ni-lv-i-s-di-yi a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi a-le u-ni-ga-do-v-sv-hi tsi-gi du-yu-go-dv-i.

150404.english: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:
150404.cherokee: ᏂᎦᎥᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏢᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏏᏳ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏲᎢᏎᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᎣᏓᏅᏛ ᏱᏛᏓᏂᎸᎦ;
150404.phonetic: Ni-ga-v-ye-no go-hu-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi u-we-tlv-nv-hi ge-sv o-si-yu, a-le v-tla go-hu-s-di a-yo-i-se-di yi-gi, i-yu-no u-li-he-li-s-di o-da-nv-dv yi-dv-da-ni-lv-ga;

150405.english: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
150405.cherokee: ᎦᏓᎭᏰᏃ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᎬᏁᎸᎯ ᎬᏔᏅᎯ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150405.phonetic: Ga-da-ha-ye-no ni-ge-sv-na i-gv-ne-lv-hi gv-ta-nv-hi ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, a-le a-da-do-li-s-do-di ge-sv-i.

150406.english: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.
150406.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᎩᏯᏅᏓᏗᏍᏔᏅ, ᎰᏍᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎡᏣᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎡᏤᎳᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏥᎧᏃᎮᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏳᎳ ᏗᏣᏂᏴᏛ ᏥᎩ.
150406.phonetic: I-yu-no a-na-li-nv-tli hi-a na-s-gi yi-gi-ya-nv-da-di-s-ta-nv, ho-s-dv ge-se-s-di e-tsa-nv-si-da-s-di Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv u-tse-li-ga, e-tse-la-s-ta-nv-hi ge-se-s-di ka-no-he-dv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv tsi-ka-no-he-ha, a-le o-s-dv di-de-yo-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi ga-yu-la di-tsa-ni-yv-dv tsi-gi.

150407.english: But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.
150407.cherokee: ᏕᏣᏲᎢᏎᎮᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᏪᏢᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᏗ ᏥᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏁᎶᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150407.phonetic: De-tsa-yo-i-se-he-s-di-s-gi-ni ga-we-tlv-di ge-sv go-hu-s-di gv-do-di ni-ge-sv-na a-le a-ni-ga-yv-li-ge u-ni-no-he-lv-di tsi-gi; a-le tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ne-lo-do-di ge-sv-i.

150408.english: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
150408.cherokee: ᎠᏰᎸᎢᏰᏃ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏳ ᎠᏓᏍᏕᎵᎭ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᏁᎶᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎬᏩᏓᏍᏕᎸᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎠᏚᎢᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᏁᏗᏱ ᎬᏅ ᎪᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᏱᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150408.phonetic: A-ye-lv-i-ye-no u-gv-wa-li di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv u-s-di-gi-yu a-da-s-de-li-ha; U-ne-la-nv-hi-s-gi-ni di-ne-lo-do-di ge-sv ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di u-gv-wa-li gv-wa-da-s-de-lv-di, na-s-gi-ye-no a-du-i-s-di a-tsi-ne-di-yi gv-nv go-hi tsi-gi, a-le na-s-gi u-li-yi-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv-i.

150409.english: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.

150410.english: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.
150410.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏕᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏰᎩᏐᏢᏗᎭ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎡᏓᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏛ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ.
150410.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no i-yu-s-di de-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha a-le ga-ye-gi-so-tlv-di-ha nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di e-da-li-s-ga-s-do-dv gv-ni-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi, na-s-gi Di-s-de-li-s-gi tsi-gi na-ni-v yv-wi, na-s-gi ho u-gv-wi-yu-ha na-s-gi na a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi.

150411.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎯᏁᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎭᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
150411.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a nu-s-de-s-di hi-ne-ge-s-di a-le de-ha-de-yo-hv-s-ge-s-di.

150412.english: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
150412.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᏳᏐᏅᏤᎴᏏᏗ ᎯᏫᏂᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎦᎨᏣᏕᎶᏆᎡᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ, ᎯᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏣᏛᎿᎭᏕᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏓᎨᏳᎡᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏓᏅᏖᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᎯᏳᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150412.phonetic: Tle-s-di gi-lo yu-so-nv-tse-le-si-di hi-wi-ni-yu ge-sv-i; ga-ge-tsa-de-lo-qua-e-di-s-gi-ni ge-se-s-di a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi, hi-wo-ni-s-gv-i a-le ni-tsa-dv-hna-de-gv-i, a-le tsa-da-ge-yu-e-i, a-le tsa-da-nv-te-di-yi, a-le tso-hi-yu-sv-i, a-le ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

150413.english: Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.
150413.cherokee: ᏫᏥᎷᏨᎭ ᎢᏯᏍᏘ ᏕᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏕᎯᎪᎵᏰᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎯᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎭᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎢ.
150413.phonetic: Wi-tsi-lu-tsv-ha i-ya-s-ti de-tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-s-di go-we-li de-hi-go-li-ye-s-gv-i, a-le de-hi-wo-ne-di-s-gv-i, a-le de-ha-de-yo-hv-s-gv-i.

150414.english: Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.
150414.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏤᎵᏒᎩ ᎡᏣᏁᎸᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏣᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏣᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᏣᏏᏔᏛ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ.
150414.phonetic: Tle-s-di u-ne-la-gi tse-li-sv-gi e-tsa-ne-lv-hi na-s-gi tsi-tsa-ha, na-s-gi e-tsa-ne-lv-hi tsi-gi a-nv-di-s-gv u-na-do-le-ho-sv a-le de-ge-tsa-si-ta-dv di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di.

150415.english: Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.
150415.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎭᏓᏅᏖᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᏕᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏣᏁᏉᏤᎲ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᏛ.
150415.phonetic: Na-s-gi de-ha-da-nv-te-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi-quo de-tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-s-di, na-s-gi-no tsa-ne-quo-tse-hv gv-ni-ge-sv nu-na-li-s-da-ne-he-s-di ni-ga-dv.

150416.english: Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee.
150416.cherokee: ᏣᏓᎦᏌᏯᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᏨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏕᎭᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎯᏍᏓᏩᏗᏎᏍᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎿᎭᏛᏁᎲ ᏨᏒ ᏔᏓᏍᏕᎸᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎨᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᏙᏘᏍᏕᎸᎯ.
150416.phonetic: Tsa-da-ga-sa-ya-s-de-s-di tsv-sv a-le nu-s-dv de-ha-de-yo-hv-s-gi-i, na-s-gi de-hi-s-da-wa-di-se-s-di; na-s-gi-ye-no hna-dv-ne-hv tsv-sv ta-da-s-de-lv-hi a-le na-s-quo na-s-gi na ge-tsa-dv-da-s-da-ne-hi do-ti-s-de-lv-hi.

150501.english: Rebuke not an elder, but intreat him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;
150501.cherokee: ᎤᏛᏐᏅᎯ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᎬᏍᎪᎸᏁᏍᏗ, ᎯᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎠᏂᏫᏅᏃ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏟ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ,
150501.phonetic: U-dv-so-nv-hi tle-s-di yi-gv-s-go-lv-ne-s-di, hi-s-di-ye-di-s-ge-s-di-s-gi-ni a-ga-yv-li-ge na-s-gi-ya-i, a-ni-wi-nv-no i-tsa-da-nv-tli na-s-gi-ya-i,

150502.english: The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.
150502.cherokee: ᎠᎾᎦᏴᎵᎨᏃ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏗᏣᏥ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎠᏂᏫᏅᎨᏃ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏤᏣᏙ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎤᏐᏅ ᏄᏓᏑᏴᎾ.
150502.phonetic: A-na-ga-yv-li-ge-no a-ni-ge-yv di-tsa-tsi na-s-gi-ya-i, a-ni-wi-nv-ge-no a-ni-ge-yv tse-tsa-do na-s-gi-ya-i, u-so-nv nu-da-su-yv-na.

150503.english: Honour widows that are widows indeed.
150503.cherokee: ᏕᎯᎸᏉᏕᏍᏗ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᏱᎩ.
150503.phonetic: De-hi-lv-quo-de-s-di tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi, i-yu-no u-do-hi-yu-sv tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi yi-gi.

150504.english: But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.
150504.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏬᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎵᏏ ᏯᏁᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏕᎶᏆᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᎸᏉᏙᏗᏱ ᏙᏧᏁᏅᏒ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᎫᏴᎡᏗᏱ ᏧᏂᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗᏳ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
150504.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni gi-lo u-wo-su-lo-tsv-hi tsu-we-tsi a-le tsu-li-si ya-ne-ha, na-s-gi a-na-de-lo-qua-s-ge-s-di tsu-ni-lv-quo-do-di-yi do-tsu-ne-nv-sv a-ne-hi, a-le tsu-na-gu-yv-e-di-yi tsu-ni-ga-yv-li-ge-i; na-s-gi-ye-no o-si-yu, a-le tsu-da-ni-lv-i-s-di-yu U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

150505.english: Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.
150505.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ Ꮎ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎤᏬᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒᎯᏳ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏐ ᎠᏔᏲᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᎵᏏᏂᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏚᎩᏨᏂᏒᎢ.
150505.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no na u-do-hi-yu-hi u-wo-su-lo-tsv-hi tsi-ge-so-i, a-le u-wa-sv-hi-yu tsi-ge-so-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-li-s-ga-s-do-di-s-go-i, a-le da-ka-hna-wa-di-so a-ta-yo-s-di ge-sv a-le a-da-do-li-s-do-di ge-sv ni-du-li-si-ni-sv a-le ni-du-gi-tsv-ni-sv-i.

150506.english: But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.
150506.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏄᏁᎸᎾ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎨᏐ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎠᎴᏂᏙᎲᎢ.
150506.phonetic: Na-s-gi na nu-ne-lv-na e-hi u-yo-hu-sv-hi ge-so a-si-quo a-le-ni-do-hv-i.

150507.english: And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless.
150507.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎯᏁᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎨᎫᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
150507.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi hi-a nu-s-de-s-di hi-ne-ge-s-di, na-s-gi ga-ge-gu-i-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

150508.english: But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.
150508.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᏓᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎭ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏚᏓᏘᎿᎭᎥᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᏱᎸ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟ ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ Ꮎ ᏄᏬᎯᏳᏒᎾ.
150508.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni-no gi-lo ni-da-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-hv-na i-ge-se-s-di u-wa-sv tsu-tse-li-ga, a-le na-s-gi ho u-gv-wi-yu-ha u-wa-sv du-da-ti-hna-v-i, na-s-gi u-da-yi-lv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-tli u-yo-i-yu e-s-ga-quo na nu-wo-hi-yu-sv-na.

150509.english: Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old, having been the wife of one man,
150509.cherokee: ᎤᏬᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎠᎦᏎᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏑᏓᎳᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎶᏒᏍᏕᏍᏗ, ᏌᏉᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎾᏁᎳᏛᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
150509.phonetic: U-wo-su-lo-tsv-hi a-ga-se-s-di ge-se-s-di i-yu-no su-da-la-s-go-hi i-yu-de-ti-yv-dv ge-se-s-di, a-le u-lo-sv-s-de-s-di, sa-quo-quo a-s-ga-ya u-na-ne-la-dv-hi ge-se-s-di.

150510.english: Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have lodged strangers, if she have washed the saints' feet, if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.
150510.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎣᏏ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏧᏛᎯᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏁᏙᎯ ᏧᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᏧᎾᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏧᏬᏑᎴᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏧᏍᏕᎸᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᎩᎵᏲᎩ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150510.phonetic: I-yu-no o-s-dv a-tsi-no-he-s-gi ge-se-s-di o-si de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, i-yu-no di-ni-yo-li tsu-dv-hi-s-ta-nv-hi ge-se-s-di, i-yu-no a-ne-do-hi tsu-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-hi ge-se-s-di, i-yu-no u-na-da-nv-ti tsu-na-la-si-de-ni tsu-wo-su-le-lv-hi ge-se-s-di, i-yu-no tsu-s-de-lv-dv ge-se-s-di a-ni-gi-li-yo-gi, i-yu-no u-s-da-wa-dv-dv ge-se-s-di ni-ga-v o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i.

150511.english: But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;
150511.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏫᏅᎨᏌᏂᏍᎩᏂ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎩᏲᎢᏎᎮᏍᏗ; ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏄᎾᏁᎸᎾ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ ᎠᏁᎲ, ᎦᎶᏁᏛᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎠᏁᎳ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎪ ᏧᎾᏨᏍᏗᏱ,
150511.phonetic: A-ni-wi-nv-ge-sa-ni-s-gi-ni tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi gi-yo-i-se-he-s-di; i-yu-ye-no nu-na-ne-lv-na ni-ga-li-s-da a-ne-hv, Ga-lo-ne-dv-no u-ne-la-gi a-ne-la, hna-quo u-na-du-li-s-go tsu-na-tsv-s-di-yi,

150512.english: Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith.
150512.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏚᎪᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏐᎢ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪ ᏕᎤᏂᏲᏒ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎢ.
150512.phonetic: Tsu-na-du-go-da-ne-lv-hi ge-so-i nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-go de-u-ni-yo-sv i-gv-yi-yi u-no-hi-yu-nv-i.

150513.english: And withal they learn to be idle, wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, speaking things which they ought not.
150513.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᎾᏕᎶᏆᏍᎪ ᎤᎾᏓᏄᎸᏗ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᏁᏙᎵᏙᎰ ᏓᏓᏁᎳᏗᏒᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏄᎸᏗᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᏂᏱᏙᎯ ᎨᏐᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏂᎳᏗᏌᏘ, ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏗᏱ.
150513.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo a-na-de-lo-qua-s-go u-na-da-nu-lv-di i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi, a-ne-do-li-do-ho da-da-ne-la-di-sv-i; v-tla a-le u-na-da-nu-lv-di-quo u-wa-sv yi-ge-so-i, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni ka-no-he-dv a-ni-yi-do-hi ge-so-i a-le u-ni-ni-la-di-sa-ti, a-ni-no-he-s-gi na-s-gi ni-du-yu-go-dv-na ge-sv u-ni-no-he-di-yi.

150514.english: I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.
150514.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ ᎠᏂᏫᏅᎨᏌᏂ ᏧᎾᏨᏍᏗᏱ, ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏧᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ, ᏧᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᏓᏂᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎾᏡᏗᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᎳᏅᏓᏕᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎬᏩᏂᏃᎮᏗᏱ.
150514.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di a-qua-du-li-ha a-ni-wi-nv-ge-sa-ni tsu-na-tsv-s-di-yi, di-ni-yo-li tsu-ni-na-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi, tsu-ni-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi da-ni-ne-lv-i, a-le gv-wa-na-tlu-di-s-gi tsu-ni-la-nv-da-de-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv u-so-nv gv-wa-ni-no-he-di-yi.

150515.english: For some are already turned aside after Satan.

150516.english: If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them relieve them, and let not the church be charged; that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.
150516.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏚᏪᎧᎮᏍᏗ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ, ᎤᏅᏒᏉ ᏗᏂᏍᏕᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏞᏍᏗᏃ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᏱᏚᏂᏓᏁᎴᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150516.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo go-hi-yu-hv-s-gi a-s-ga-ya a-le a-ge-yv du-we-ka-he-s-di tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi, u-nv-sv-quo di-ni-s-de-li-s-ge-s-di, tle-s-di-no tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv yi-du-ni-da-ne-le-s-di, na-s-gi di-gv-wa-ni-s-de-lv-di yi-gi u-do-hi-yu-sv tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi ge-sv-i.

150517.english: Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.
150517.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᏔᎵ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ ᏕᏥᎸᏉᏕᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ.
150517.phonetic: A-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di tsi-gi o-s-dv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi ta-li i-yu-wa-gu-di de-tsi-lv-quo-de-s-di, a-le na-s-gi ho u-gv-wi-yu-se-s-di ka-no-he-dv a-le di-de-yo-di ge-sv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi.

150518.english: For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer is worthy of his reward.
150518.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᏛᎭ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎪᏪᎵ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏲᏴᏑᎳᏁᏍᏗ ᏩᎦ ᎠᎦᏔᏙᎥᏗᏍᎩ; ᎠᎴ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏛᏁᎯ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᎨᏐ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
150518.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no ni-ga-dv-ha ga-lv-quo-di go-we-li; Tle-s-di yi-yo-yv-su-la-ne-s-di wa-ga a-ga-ta-do-v-di-s-gi; a-le, Go-hu-s-di a-dv-ne-hi a-ga-gu-yv-e-di ge-so du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i.

150519.english: Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.
150519.cherokee: ᏣᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎳᏫᏎᎲᎢ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏣᏛᏓᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎬᏂ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏦᎢ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
150519.phonetic: Tsa-ga-ne-lo-di go-hu-s-di a-tsi-la-wi-se-hv-i tle-s-di yi-tsa-dv-da-s-di-s-ge-s-di gv-ni a-ni-ta-li a-le a-ni-tso-i u-ni-no-he-dv ge-se-s-di.

150520.english: Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.
150520.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏅᎩ ᏕᎯᎬᏍᎪᎸᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏐᎢ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᎾᏰᏍᏗᏱ.
150520.phonetic: Na-no a-ni-s-ga-nv-gi de-hi-gv-s-go-lv-v-s-ge-s-di ni-ga-dv a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, na-s-gi a-ni-so-i na-s-quo u-ni-na-ye-s-di-yi.

150521.english: I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.
150521.cherokee: ᎬᏁᏤᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎨᎦᏑᏰᏛ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᎦᏌᏯᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎦᏰᎯ ᏂᏚᎪᏗᏍᎬᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎩᎶ ᏂᏯᎵᎪᏁᎲᎾ.
150521.phonetic: Gv-ne-tse-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i, a-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv a-le ge-ga-su-ye-dv di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, hi-a na-s-gi tsa-ga-sa-ya-s-do-di-yi i-gv-yi ga-ye-hi ni-du-go-di-s-gv-na, a-le de-tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv gi-lo ni-ya-li-go-ne-hv-na.

150522.english: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins: keep thyself pure.
150522.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏗ ᏱᏘᏯᏏᏔᏕᏍᏗ ᎩᎶᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏯᏖᎳᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏅᎬᎢ.  ᏣᏓᎦᏌᏯᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎯᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
150522.phonetic: Tle-s-di u-li-s-di yi-ti-ya-si-ta-de-s-di gi-lo-i, a-le tle-s-di ya-te-la-di-s-ge-s-di gi-lo a-ni-s-ga-nv-gv-i.  Tsa-da-ga-sa-ya-s-de-s-di hi-ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na ge-se-s-di.

150523.english: Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities.
150523.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎠᎹᏉ ᏯᏗᏔᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎭᏗᏔᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎭᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏦᎸᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏯᏃᏉ ᏣᏢᏥᏰᏍᎬᎢ.
150523.phonetic: Hna-quo tle-s-di a-ma-quo ya-di-ta-s-ge-s-di, u-s-di-s-gi-ni gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di ha-di-ta-s-ge-s-di, go-hu-s-di ha-li-s-di-s-gv i-yu-s-di tso-lv-hi-s-di-yi a-le ya-no-quo tsa-tlv-tsi-ye-s-gv-i.

150524.english: Some men's sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they follow after.
150524.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏅᎬ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎨᏐ ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᏫᎦᎷᎪ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎤᎨᏍᏓᏩᏕᎪᎢ.
150524.phonetic: Gi-lo a-ni-s-ga-nv-gv gv-ni-ge-sv ge-so i-gv-we-nv-dv, i-gv-yi wi-ga-lu-go di-gu-go-do-di-yi ge-sv-i, gi-lo-no u-ge-s-da-wa-de-go-i.

150525.english: Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.
150525.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎩᎶ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎨᏐ ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏛᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᏎ Ꮭ ᎦᎬᏍᎦᎶᏗ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ.
150525.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo gi-lo o-s-dv du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv gv-ni-ge-sv ge-so i-gv-we-nv-dv; gi-lo-no na-s-gi na hi-a nu-s-dv-na yi-gi, a-se tla ga-gv-s-ga-lo-di yi-ge-so-i.

150601.english: Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed.
150601.cherokee: ᎾᏂᎥ ᏗᎨᏥᎾᏝᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎨᏥᎩᎳᎾᎳ ᏥᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏂᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎾᏝᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏐᏢᎯ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
150601.phonetic: Na-ni-v di-ge-tsi-na-tla-i na-s-gi di-ge-tsi-gi-la-na-la tsi-gi u-tsa-ti tsu-ni-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se-s-di di-gv-wa-ni-na-tla-i, na-s-gi du-do-v U-ne-la-nv-hi a-le u-tse-li di-de-yo-di ge-sv a-so-tlv-hi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

150602.english: And they that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brethren; but rather do them service, because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.
150602.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎾᏝᎢ ᎨᏒ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏚᏂᏂᏆᏘᎴᏍᏗ ᏱᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᏓᎾᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᎨᏳᎢ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᎾᏠᏯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎾᎦᏛᏁᎸᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᏕᎭᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎯᏁᏤᎮᏍᏗ.
150602.phonetic: Na-no a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi di-gv-wa-ni-na-tla-i ge-sv tle-s-di yi-du-ni-ni-qua-ti-le-s-di yi-nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di a-na-li-nv-tli ge-sv-i, go-hu-s-di-s-gi-ni da-na-dv-ne-he-s-di nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi a-le ge-tsi-ge-yu-i ge-sv-i, u-na-tlo-ya-s-di-s-gi o-s-dv na-ga-dv-ne-lv-i.  Na-s-gi hi-a nu-s-de-s-di de-ha-de-yo-hv-s-ge-s-di, a-le de-hi-ne-tse-he-s-di.

150603.english: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;
150603.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏏ ᎾᏰᎸᏍᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏬᏂᏒ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏂᎶᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏥᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎦ;
150603.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo u-tsa-ti-ni ni-ga-we-s-ge-s-di da-de-yo-hv-s-ge-s-di, a-le o-si na-ye-lv-s-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di o-s-dv u-wo-ni-sv i-ga-tse-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv, a-le di-de-yo-di ge-sv na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ni-lo-do-di ge-sv tsi-da-de-yo-hv-s-ga;

150604.english: He is proud, knowing nothing, but doting about questions and strifes of words, whereof cometh envy, strife, railings, evil surmisings,
150604.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏢᏉᏗ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᎦᏔᎲᎾ, ᎤᎵᏰᏔᏁᎯ ᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏘᏲᏌᏘ ᎠᎵᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏨᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏒᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᏓᏕᎵᏎᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ,
150604.phonetic: Na-s-gi na u-tlv-quo-di, go-hu-s-di na-ga-ta-hv-na, u-li-ye-ta-ne-hi ga-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv, a-le tsu-ti-yo-sa-ti a-li-no-he-s-gv-i, na-hna tsv-di-da-le-hv-s-ga a-dv-yu-ge-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-di-sv-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-so-tlv-i-s-do-di ge-sv-i, a-le go-hu-s-di di-da-de-li-se-di ge-sv-i,

150605.english: Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.
150605.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏍᎦᎢᏍᏓᎩ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎪᏏᏛ ᏧᎾᏓᏅᏘ, ᎤᏂᏂᎩᏛ ᏚᏳᎪᏛᎢ, ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏁᎶᏙᏗ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎩ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏕᎭᏓᏅᎡᎮᏍᏗ.
150605.phonetic: A-le gv-s-ga-i-s-da-gi u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv yv-wi u-go-si-dv tsu-na-da-nv-ti, u-ni-ni-gi-dv du-yu-go-dv-i, ge-nv-i-s-di ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ne-lo-do-di i-tsu-la-ha a-ne-li-s-gi.  Na-s-gi i-yu-na-s-di de-ha-da-nv-e-he-s-di.

150606.english: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
150606.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᏁᎶᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ.
150606.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi-s-gi-ni di-ne-lo-do-di ge-sv a-le nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv a-da-nv-da-di-s-di ge-sv, na-s-gi u-tsa-ti ge-nv-i-s-di.

150607.english: For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.
150607.cherokee: ᏝᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᎩᏲᎴ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏳᎢᏳ Ꮭ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏴᎨᏗᏄᎪᏩ.
150607.phonetic: Tla-ye-no go-hu-s-di yi-gi-yo-le a-ni e-lo-hi, a-le gv-ni-ge-yu-i-yu tla go-hu-s-di yv-ge-di-nu-go-wa.

150608.english: And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.
150608.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᏱᎩᎭ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏄᏬ ᏱᏗᎩᎾᎠ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎢᎩᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ.
150608.phonetic: I-yu-no yi-gi-ha a-li-s-da-yv-di a-le di-ga-nu-wo yi-di-gi-na-a, na-s-gi ye-li-quo i-gi-ye-lv-se-s-di.

150609.english: But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.
150609.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᏥᎩ ᎤᏁᏅᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏓᎴᎾᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏌᏛ ᏫᏓᏂᏅᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᎩ ᎤᎵᏍᎦᏂᏍᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏯᏓᏛᏁᎯ ᎠᎬᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏚᏂᏃᏴᏘᏍᏗᎭ ᏴᏫ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150609.phonetic: U-na-du-li-s-gi-s-gi-ni tsi-gi u-ne-nv-i-s-di-yi, u-da-le-na-s-di-yi a-le a-sa-dv wi-da-ni-nv-go-i, a-le u-tsa-ti i-yu-da-le-gi u-li-s-ga-ni-s-dv a-le u-yo i-ya-da-dv-ne-hi a-gv-v-hi-s-di ge-sv-i; na-s-gi tsi-du-ni-no-yv-ti-s-di-ha yv-wi na-hna a-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-sv a-le a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i.

150610.english: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
150610.cherokee: ᎠᏕᎸᏰᏃ ᏗᎨᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ ᏄᏓᎴᏒ ᎤᏲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏂᎬᎥᏍᎬᎢ ᎤᎾᏞᏒ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏚᎾᏓᏘᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150610.phonetic: A-de-lv-ye-no di-ge-yu-di ge-sv na-hna di-da-le-hv-s-ga nu-da-le-sv u-yo-i, na-s-gi gi-lo u-ni-gv-v-s-gv-i u-na-tle-sv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-nv-sv du-na-da-ti-s-ta-nv u-tsa-ti u-yo a-da-nv-da-di-s-di ge-sv-i.

150611.english: But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.
150611.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏕᎭᏓᏅᎡᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎯᏍᏓᏩᏗᏎᏍᏗ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᎨᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
150611.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni hi-s-ga-ya U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga na-s-gi hi-a de-ha-da-nv-e-he-s-di, a-le de-hi-s-da-wa-di-se-s-di du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-na-ye-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-da-ge-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le gv-ni-di-yu ge-sv-i, a-le u-da-nv-ti-yu ge-sv-i.

150612.english: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses.
150612.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎲᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎭᎵᎮᏍᏗ.  ᎾᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏘᏂᏴ, ᎾᎿᎭᎾᏍᏉ ᎡᏣᏯᏅᏛ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏃᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᎠᏂᏚᏔᎲᎢ.
150612.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv hv-di-s-ge-s-di o-s-dv a-li-s-di ge-sv ha-li-he-s-di.  Na-li-s-qua-di-s-gv-na gv-ni-dv ge-sv ti-ni-yv, na-hna na-s-quo e-tsa-ya-nv-dv tsi-gi, a-le tsa-no-nv-hi tsi-gi o-s-dv ka-no-he-di ge-sv u-ni-tsa-ti a-ni-ga-ta-hi a-ni-du-ta-hv-i.

150613.english: I give thee charge in the sight of God, who quickeneth all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession;
150613.cherokee: ᎬᏁᏤᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏂᏗᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏄᎾᏓᎴᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏔᎲ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏆᏂᏗ ᏆᎴᏗ ᎠᎦᏔᎲ ᎤᏃᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ,
150613.phonetic: Gv-ne-tse-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i, na-s-gi di-gv-ni-di-s-gi tsi-gi ni-ga-v nu-na-da-le-sv-i, a-le a-ga-ta-hv Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv na-s-gi Qua-ni-di Qua-le-di a-ga-ta-hv u-no-nv-hi tsi-gi o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv ge-sv-i,

150614.english: That thou keep this commandment without spot, unrebukeable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ:
150614.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ, ᏄᏓᏓᎸᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᏰᏤᎵᏎᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏥᎸ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ,
150614.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsa-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di, nu-da-da-lv-na a-le go-hu-s-di ga-ye-tse-li-se-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi i-ga-tse-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv ga-na-nu-go-tsi-lv gv-di-s-gi,

150615.english: Which in his times he shall shew, who is the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;
150615.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏨᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏ ᎾᎯᏳ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒᎯ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ;
150615.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsv-ga-na-nu-go-wi-si na-hi-yu tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv u-da-nv-ti a-le u-wa-sv-hi u-li-ni-gi-dv ge-sv-i, U-gv-wi-yu-hi U-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi a-ne-hv-i, a-le U-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi Nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-ne-hv-i;

150616.english: Who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto; whom no man hath seen, nor can see: to whom be honour and power everlasting. Amen.
150616.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒᎯ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎯ, ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎡᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎾᎿᎭᎩᎶ ᎬᏩᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏫᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
150616.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-wa-sv-hi a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv u-hi, i-ga-ga-dv e-hi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi i-ga-ga-dv na-hna gi-lo gv-wa-lu-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, na-s-gi gi-lo u-go-hv-gi ni-ge-sv-na, a-le gv-wa-go-wa-dv-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i; na-s-gi a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se-s-di a-le u-li-ni-gi-di ge-sv wi-na-s-dv-na ge-se-s-di.  E-me-nv.

150617.english: Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;
150617.cherokee: ᎩᏁᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᏧᏁᎿᎭᎢ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏢᏉᏗ ᏱᏉ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᏓᎵᏓᏍᏗ ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎢᎩᏁᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎾᎦᎥ ᏄᏓᎴᏒ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᎦᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ.
150617.phonetic: Gi-ne-tse-he-s-di tsu-ne-hna-i tsi-gi a-ni e-lo-hi na-s-gi u-na-tlv-quo-di yi-quo ni-ge-sv-na, a-le u-na-li-s-ga-s-do-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na u-da-li-da-s-di ge-nv-i-s-di ge-sv-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi-s-gi-ni gv-ni-dv a-na-li-s-ga-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di na-s-gi u-tsa-ti i-gi-ne-hi tsi-gi na-ga-v nu-da-le-sv o-s-dv i-ga-li-s-da-ne-di.

150618.english: That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate;
150618.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎿᎭᎢᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎦᎵᏰᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏁᏗᏱ, ᎠᏂᏯᏙᎯᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎲᎢ,
150618.phonetic: A-le o-s-dv na-na-dv-ne-he-s-di, a-le u-ne-hna-i-yu ge-se-s-di o-s-dv na-na-dv-ne-hv-i, a-le a-ni-ga-li-ye-s-di u-na-da-ne-di-yi, a-ni-ya-do-hi-hi ge-se-s-di u-ni-hv-i,

150619.english: Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.
150619.cherokee: ᎠᎾᏓᏁᎸᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎦᎫᏍᏛᏗ ᎠᏏ ᎾᏍᏆᎵᏍᎬᎾ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏂᏴᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
150619.phonetic: A-na-da-ne-lv-s-ge-s-di u-nv-sv u-na-tse-li-ga o-s-dv ga-gu-s-dv-di a-si na-s-qua-li-s-gv-na na-hi-yu u-s-qua-lv-di tsi-gi, na-s-gi na-li-s-qua-di-s-gv-na gv-ni-dv di-gv-wa-ni-ni-yv-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

150620.english: O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane and vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called:
150620.cherokee: ᏗᎹᏗ, ᏣᏍᏆᏂᎪᏕᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏤᏣᎨᏅᏴ, ᎢᏴᏛ ᏅᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎦᏪᏢᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏒᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᏥᏚᏙᎥ;
150620.phonetic: Di-ma-di, tsa-s-qua-ni-go-de-s-di na-s-gi tse-tsa-ge-nv-yv, i-yv-dv nv-ne-he-s-di ga-we-tlv-di ge-sv a-le a-se-quo-quo ga-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-di-sv-s-di ge-sv a-ga-do-v-hi-s-di ni-du-yu-go-dv-na tsi-du-do-v;

150621.english: Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.
150621.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏂᏍᏓᏩᏗᏒ ᎤᎾᏞᏒ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.  [ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ] ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏕᏣᎧᏩᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
150621.phonetic: Na-s-gi gi-lo a-ni-s-da-wa-di-sv u-na-tle-sv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i.  [U-ne-la-nv-hi] u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv de-tsa-ka-wa-di-do-he-s-di.  E-me-nv.
